title: speed up d1->tinybird sync to every 15min
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Clockwork data syncs
gist.blurb: We've cranked up our internal data syncs to run every 15 minutes instead of hourly. Fresher data for the team means faster support for the hive, all without touching the live database.
gist.summary: Updated the D1 to Tinybird synchronization cron job to run every 15 minutes instead of hourly.
gist.impact: The support and development teams now get significantly fresher data snapshots for debugging and analytics without requiring direct access to the live database.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and a tiny pixel bird (representing Tinybird) working together to move glowing data cubes between two retro server racks. A chunky digital clock or stopwatch emphasizes the 15-minute speed.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee and a small blue bird quickly moving glowing data cubes between two retro server racks. A chunky digital clock on the wall reads '15:00'. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes.
